one_time_description: Transfer to capital assets as prior year restatement adjustment increasing accumulated surplus opening balance
economic_notes: Halalt First Nation has investments in 1241478 BC LTD, Halalt Corporate Holdings LTD, VIG Projects BC GP LTD, VIG Projects BC Limited Partnership (100% interest), and Halalt Logging Contracting Limited Partnership (99.9% interest). Also has cost-basis investments in Hul'q'umi'num Fisheries Limited Partnership and BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing Limited Partnership. Economic development expenses of $1,038,429 reported.
notes: Audit opinion is QUALIFIED due to: (1) management switched accounting systems and certain account balances and department surplus/deficit were changed without adequate supporting records — accumulated surplus/deficit by department is undetermined; (2) First Nation was unable to provide information for certain revenue and government funding, so revenues, accounts receivable, and deferred revenue may be understated or overstated. Opening accumulated surplus was restated upward by $3,826,557 for transfer to capital assets and $31,270 prior year adjustment. Deferred capital contributions of $1,604,499 eliminated in 2022 (was present in 2021). Current liabilities calculated as accounts payable $330,860 plus current portion of long-term debt $73,556. Long-term debt shown as $888,444 non-current portion. Total_government_transfers includes ISC $3,748,504 + Other government funding $1,424,752 + Province of BC $967,444 + DFO $264,194 + CMHC $14,191 - note DFO included as government transfer. OSR includes Shellfish Sales, Rental income, Interest income, Ottawa Trust, Other income.
